text.title: Rails Decomposition Failure Modes
text.summary: 6 antipatterns observed in real Rails codebases applying the decomposition pattern. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: `Users::CreateService`, `Users::UpdateService`, `Users::DeleteService` â€” three nearly-identical service classes copied from a template.
antipattern.root-cause: Agents created one service per HTTP verb instead of consolidating CRUD variants.
antipattern.fix: Consolidate variations into a single `Users::PersistenceService` with conditional logic; reserve multiple services for distinct domain operations, not HTTP verbs.

antipattern.symptom: Some services return `User`, some return `Result.new(ok:, errors:)`, some raise; controllers handle each pattern differently and miss edge cases.

root-cause.source: Avdi Grimm â€” Object on Rails

antipattern.root-cause: No canonical return shape locked in `CONTRIBUTING.md`.
antipattern.fix: Pick one (Result vs entity-or-raise), document it, and enforce via code review + a static check (`bundle exec rubocop --only ServiceReturnShape`).

antipattern.symptom: `app/models/concerns/searchable.rb` is 320 LOC and mixes search, slug, soft-delete, audit logic.
antipattern.root-cause: Concerns used as a dumping ground for unrelated cross-cutting logic.
antipattern.fix: Concerns stay narrow mixins (1-20 LOC) for one single cross-cutting behaviour. Complex logic belongs in services or query objects.

antipattern.symptom: The same validation appears in both `Users::SignupForm` and `Users::CreateService`; agents update one and miss the other.
antipattern.root-cause: No clear contract between form-shape validation and business-rule validation.
antipattern.fix: Form Objects validate input shape only (presence, format). Services validate business rules. Refactor overlaps into a single shape contract.

antipattern.symptom: `User.where(active: true)` inside a query object AND `scope :active` on the model; one site updates, the other doesn't.
antipattern.root-cause: Query Object re-implemented an existing scope.
antipattern.fix: Query Objects compose existing scopes (`User.active.recent.where(...)`); no inline filter duplication.

antipattern.symptom: Counter caches stale; audit log missing entries; agent reached for `update_columns` "for performance".
antipattern.root-cause: Service bypassed callbacks via `update_columns`, breaking cross-cutting behaviour.
antipattern.fix: Document which callbacks fire on which methods. Flag `update_columns` / `update_all` in code review; require an explicit `# @warning bypasses callbacks` comment.
